question: Explain the importance of Design Systems in Detailed Design phase.
expectedPoints: Design systems ensure consistency across the product
They provide reusable components saving time
They bridge gap between designers and developers
They establish clear guidelines for visual and interaction patterns

question: What are Design Tokens? Give examples.
expectedPoints: Design tokens are atomic visual properties stored as variables
Examples include colors, fonts, spacing values, and border radii
They enable cross-platform consistency
They can be referenced in code without hardcoding values

question: List and explain four key principles of Visual Hierarchy.
expectedPoints: Size - larger elements attract more attention
Color - contrasting colors stand out more
Spacing - isolated elements draw focus
Alignment - creates visual connections between related items

question: What is the purpose of Design Handoff? List any three items included in design handoff documentation.
expectedPoints: Purpose: Transfer precise specifications to developers for accurate implementation
Items: Measurement specifications (pixels, rem)
Color codes and hex values
Asset files (images, icons, fonts)
Interaction specifications and animations

question: Explain the CRAP principle in visual design with examples.
expectedPoints: C - Contrast: Using different colors/sizes to show difference (black text on white background)
R - Repetition: Repeating styles for consistency (same button style across pages)
A - Alignment: Aligning elements to create visual connection (left-aligned text)
P - Proximity: Grouping related items together (form fields grouped under labels)

question: What is Responsive Design? Explain any two techniques used in responsive design.
expectedPoints: Responsive design adapts layouts to different screen sizes
Technique 1: Media queries - CSS rules that apply based on viewport width
Technique 2: Flexible grid layouts - using percentages instead of fixed widths
Technique 3: Flexible images - images that scale within their containers

question: Discuss the accessibility considerations in Detailed Design. What WCAG guidelines should designers follow?
expectedPoints: Explain why accessibility matters - inclusive design for all users
Contrast ratios - minimum 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text
Text alternatives - alt text for images, labels for form inputs
Keyboard navigation - all functions accessible via keyboard
Focus indicators - visible focus states for interactive elements
Color independence - information not conveyed by color alone
Semantic HTML - proper heading hierarchy and landmarks
Consideration for cognitive accessibility - clear language, consistent navigation

question: Explain the process of creating a comprehensive Style Guide for a web application. What components should be included?
expectedPoints: Introduction and purpose of style guide
Color palette - primary, secondary, accent colors with hex/RGB values
Typography - font families, sizes, weights, line heights for each level
Spacing system - consistent margins, paddings using a scale
Component specifications - buttons, forms, cards with all states
Grid system - column structure and responsive breakpoints
Iconography - icon style, sizes, usage guidelines
Motion guidelines - animation timing, transitions, microinteractions
Accessibility standards - contrast requirements, keyboard support
Usage examples and do's/don'ts

question: What is Color Theory in UI design? Explain different color schemes and their appropriate usage.
expectedPoints: Color theory explains how colors interact and affect users
Primary colors: Red, Blue, Yellow - base colors
Secondary colors: Green, Orange, Purple - created by mixing primaries
Complementary: Opposite colors - creates high contrast, use for CTAs
Analogous: Adjacent colors - harmonious, use for backgrounds, groupings
Triadic: Three equidistant colors - vibrant, use for variety
Monochromatic: Single color with variations - cohesive, elegant
Psychology: Blue for trust, Red for urgency, Green for success
Practical tips: Limit palette to 3-5 colors, ensure accessibility

question: Explain Typography Scale and its importance in Detailed Design. How do you create an effective typography system?
expectedPoints: Typography scale ensures consistent, harmonious text sizing
Modular scale approach - each level relates to previous by ratio (1.2, 1.25, 1.414)
Type scale typically includes: H1 (largest), H2, H3, H4, body, small text
Line height: 1.4-1.6 for body, 1.1-1.3 for headings
Font weight: Regular (400), Bold (700) for hierarchy
Font pairing: Choose complementary fonts - one for headings, one for body
Responsive type: Adjust sizes for different breakpoints
Use rem/em units for scalability
Establish clear hierarchy and consistent usage throughout
